channel.title: The Brussels Times
channel.description: Latest Brussels posts from The Brussels Times.

item.title: 'I paid €49 for speed dating in Brussels, and it was disastrous'
item.description: One Brussels resident recounts her "disastrous" experience of speedy dating in the city – which has put her off from ever going again.

item.title: 'Why scrap this? We want to work': The Brussels cleaners under threat from new funding reforms
item.description: Companies in the domestic services sector have long been able to receive funding for social employment integration, but as of next year this will change – prompting fears of lay-offs.

item.title: Iconic piece of Brussels brewing heritage is under threat
item.description: Local campaigners have sounded the alarm over a renovation project involving an emblematic old Brussels brewery on the canal.

item.title: Dear Kristina: 'Is office lunch still a thing in Brussels?'
item.description: Up until 10 years ago, lunchtime for office workers was a communal affair. Around 13:00, someone would push their chair back and say, “Fancy lunch?”.

item.title: Stepping into summer: Up to 30°C in Brussels next week
item.description: As summer edges closer, Brussels residents may be feeling despondent about the lack of sunshine and warm temperatures – but better weather is on the horizon.

item.title: Brussels' American theatre to become Flemish cultural hub
item.description: Built in 1958 for the World's Fair, the theatre was used by VRT mainly for studio recordings after Expo 58.

item.title: Brussels' Avenue de Stalingrad to be redeveloped this autumn
item.description: The avenue has been impacted for several years by construction on Metro Line 3 and the Toots Thielemans station.

item.title: Angry Brussels residents protest against social housing project on green land
item.description: One of the last open-field areas in Brussels is set to be built on with a new social housing project run by the region.

item.title: Catcalling and staring: Women demand safe running routes in Brussels
item.description: Of the 65% of women who say they have experienced aggression in a survey, fewer than 2% actually report it, as they do not consider it "serious enough".

item.title: Brussels to get a second Car-Free Sunday next year despite date row
item.description: Brussels residents can count on a second Car-Free Sunday in 2027 – but a row has broken out over the proposed new date.

item.title: 'Undertourism'? Brussels residents feel city is underrated by visitors
item.description: While many European capitals are seeking to curb the influx of visitors, the majority of Brussels residents believe that their city is underrated.

item.title: Hantavirus: One more 'contact case' identified in Brussels
item.description: The public health risk in Belgium is "very low" for the general population, according to the Health Ministry.

item.title: VUB professor becomes first Belgian to land top position at CERN
item.description: VUB Professor Steven Lowette has been appointed as physics coordinator at the European Organisation for Nuclear Research, known as CERN, starting September.

item.title: Police seize counterfeit goods across five sites in Brussels North
item.description: Shoes, bags, perfumes, and cigarettes were among the items seized in a Brussels-North police operation last month.

item.title: Brussels Manneken Pis's wardrobe relocates to different museum
item.description: Nearly 1,200 costumes currently stored at the Musée de la Ville de Bruxelles will be moved to the Museum of Fashion and Lace.

item.title: 'No place for hate in our streets': Brussels Pride performers attacked in broad daylight
item.description: The artists were verbally and physically attacked in the city centre after performing at Brussels Pride on Saturday.

item.title: Dutch-language schools in Brussels are short on places again
item.description: More families have registered for Dutch-language secondary education in Brussels than there are places available, with a total of 959 children missing out.

item.title: Quintin calls on Flanders to take responsibility at Brussels Airport
item.description: Interior Minister Bernard Quintin (MR) is urging Flanders to invest in the airport's infrastructure to tackle long queues, amid border police shortage.

item.title: Brussels hospital joins new evacuation plan for war or natural disasters
item.description: The project aims to prepare Belgium for medical evacuations during major events including military conflicts and natural disasters.

item.title: Brussels brasserie to host street party with food under €2
item.description: If you're strapped for cash at the end of the month and need a budget dinner before payday, then one Brussels brasserie has just the ticket.
